metadata.title:
UNDER NEW HEAD $50, 600 CLUB BEGINS


LAST LAP IN MARATHON TO MAKE GOOD

Dat With Uncle Sans Checking Up Result Five
Years BOOSTING

pages.raw_text:
Officers and members of the One
Hundred and Fifty Thousand club
0f Spokane, conceded to be the
foremost booster" organization of
it, kind in the Pacific Northwest,
have entered upon the sixth year of
activity, headed by c. Herbert
Moore, who will take up the COM
bined position of president and
general manager in few days. in
the spirit of public enterprise and
without salary or other pecuniary
reward. The other members of the
board of managers for 1910 are:
John F. Meagher, vice president:
w. D. Vincent. treasurer; Harry j.
Nicely, F. Wallace King, H. E. Os-
valid, J. l.. Paine, James c. Cum
ningham. Phil T. Becher, S. A.
Mann, Arthur w. Jones, Gordon c.
Corbaley. Gus Meese, M. H. Eggles-
ton and j. Oscar Peterson. Miss
Mildred Simmons is assistant Sec
rotary.


MOORE HAS DONE MUCH FOR


"clTY BEAUTIFUL"


Mr. Moore has been identified
with public enterprises in Spokane
for years. He is one of the pioneers
of the city and 20 years ago was
superintendent of a little cable
railway, since which time he has
been in the hotel business, in the
furniture trade and served as
mayor from 1907 to 1909. He re-
tired from active business several
years ago to devote his time to the
improvement of property interests.
He was one of the prime movers in
the old Spokane exposition, which
was destroyed by fire IA years ago.
He has always been identified with
the parks and boulevards move
ment, and while mayor of Spokane
he devoted much time to this
branch of the administration. He
was one of the first members of
the City Beautiful" committee of
the club and worked constantly and
consistently for a cleaner, brighter
and more progressive municipality.
10000 CLUB A GREAT INFLU-


ENCE.


The One Hundred and Fifty
Thousand club, organized five
years ago, has 5000 members, and
has figured at all times as an i'm
portant ally of the manufacturing
and commercial interests of the
city. While its membership en-
braces men in all walks of life, it
